macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US industrial output rises on AI-related investment, while broader manufacturing weakens
items.summary: April US industrial production rose 0.7% month over month and 1.4% year over year, beating expectations, but the gains were concentrated in business equipment tied to AI data-center investment. Consumer goods manufacturing declined and construction stalled, suggesting the factory-sector rebound remains narrow.

items.title: Japan producer prices hit record high as inflation pressures persist
items.summary: Japan’s producer price index climbed 4.9% in April from a year earlier, an all-time high in data going back to 1960 and well above expectations. The report adds to evidence that input-cost inflation remains strong in Japan, even as policymakers assess the durability of domestic price pressures.

items.title: Japan machine tool orders surge to record levels
items.summary: Machine tool orders in Japan jumped 45% year over year in April, far exceeding forecasts and matching March’s all-time-high level. Both domestic and foreign orders accelerated, signaling firm industrial demand despite global trade uncertainty.

items.title: Bond markets reassess US outlook amid shifting growth and policy expectations
items.summary: The briefing says market participants are rethinking the US outlook, with the 10-year Treasury yield around 4.60% and investors weighing stronger industrial data against broader economic weakness. The move reflects a wider adjustment in fixed-income views on growth, inflation, and policy risks.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto market sees heavy liquidations as Bitcoin drops below $77K
items.summary: Bitcoin slid to around $76,700 and the broader crypto market sold off, triggering roughly $660 million in liquidations over 24 hours. Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin, and other major tokens also moved lower amid the risk-off shift.

items.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP and Dogecoin fall after Trump warning to Iran
items.summary: Crypto prices declined after President Donald Trump’s warning to Iran rattled broader markets, with Bitcoin briefly falling below $77,000. Coinglass data cited in coverage showed more than $580 million liquidated in just four hours, with long positions taking most of the hit.

items.title: Crypto market liquidations top $85 million in 24 hours
items.summary: ChainCatcher, citing Coinglass, reported that the cryptocurrency market experienced about $85.34 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours. The report underscores elevated leverage and continued volatility across major tokens.

items.title: Bitcoin crash wipes out more than $660 million in leveraged positions
items.summary: Another market update said Bitcoin’s fall below $77,000 helped erase over $660 million in leveraged positions within 24 hours. Ethereum positions alone accounted for more than $257 million in liquidations, signaling broad stress in derivatives markets.

items.title: Crypto coverage highlights price weakness, while some altcoins show relative resilience
items.summary: Market analysis noted Bitcoin’s drop toward the mid-$76,000s while Ethereum and XRP held relatively better than BTC during the selloff. The report framed $75,000 as a key support zone to watch if downside pressure continues.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Sony’s Project Ace robot reportedly beats elite human table tennis player
items.summary: Sony’s Project Ace has created a robot that can defeat an elite human table tennis player, using nine cameras to analyze spin and track the ball in real time. The development highlights continued progress in fast-response robotics and embodied AI.

items.title: Ernst & Young withdraws AI-tainted cybersecurity study over fabricated data and citations
items.summary: Ernst & Young reportedly pulled back a cybersecurity study after researchers flagged fabricated data, incorrect citations, and references to reports that do not exist. The incident underscores growing concerns about AI hallucinations in corporate research and consulting workflows.

items.title: AI hardware boom gains momentum as industry shifts toward physical systems
items.summary: A recent interview and discussion highlighted a broader AI hardware boom, with emphasis on memory supply constraints, robotics, and the geopolitical importance of supply chains. The conversation also noted that humanoid robots remain largely prototype-stage, with mass deployment still gated by hardware limitations.

items.title: AI companions continue spreading as emotional use cases expand
items.summary: A discussion on intimacy and connection in the age of AI said there were more than 300 AI companion apps released in 2025, reflecting rapid growth in consumer-facing emotional AI. The trend suggests AI is increasingly being used for companionship, grief support, and other emotionally sensitive interactions.

items.title: New AI claims-processing system showcases automation trend in enterprise workflows
items.summary: A recent project demonstration showed how generative AI, LangChain, and OpenAI can be combined to automate insurance claims processing, cutting manual work from roughly 30 hours to 4-5 hours. It reflects a broader industry trend toward agentic AI and workflow automation in enterprise operations.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Virtuos says it wants to bring GTA 5 and Red Dead Redemption 2 to Switch
items.summary: Port specialist Virtuos said it is interested in bringing major Rockstar titles like Grand Theft Auto 5 and Red Dead Redemption 2 to Nintendo Switch. The studio previously handled the Switch port of L.A. Noire, reinforcing its handheld-port credentials.

items.title: Subnautica 2 reportedly sells 2 million in 12 hours
items.summary: Subnautica 2 had an unusually strong launch, with reported sales of 2 million copies in its first 12 hours and more than 600,000 concurrent players. The game’s early success stands out despite its troubled development cycle.

items.title: Lego 2K Drive delisting deadline nears
items.summary: 2K Drive is being delisted three years to the day after its original release, with only a short window left to buy it before removal. The move has drawn attention because of how soon it is being pulled from sale.

items.title: Nintendo Switch Online adds more Virtual Boy titles
items.summary: Nintendo’s latest update has already brought most of the Virtual Boy library to Switch Online, with five new titles added to the service. It is one of the more unusual retro additions to Nintendo’s subscription catalog.

items.title: Best Buy affiliate leak sparks GTA 6 preorder speculation
items.summary: Best Buy reportedly told affiliates that GTA 6 pre-orders would begin next week, fueling speculation that a new trailer could be imminent. The timing has also raised interest because Take-Two’s financial results are due soon.

items.title: Nintendo brings Zelda movie release forward
items.summary: After previously being delayed, The Legend of Zelda movie has now been moved up to an earlier release date. Shigeru Miyamoto confirmed the film will arrive in April.

items.title: Tomodachi Life stays No. 1 in Japan for a fourth week
items.summary: Tomodachi Life remained at the top of Japan’s weekly game rankings for the fourth consecutive week and crossed 1 million units sold. The chart also highlighted strong ongoing sales for several Nintendo titles.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the web
items.summary: Amazon is expanding Alexa+ beyond devices and onto the web, signaling a push toward agentic workflows and tighter competition with ChatGPT and Google Gemini. The move broadens access to Amazon's assistant as the AI-influenced consumer software race heats up.

items.title: Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 ends with $1.3M in bug bounties
items.summary: Researchers earned about $1.3 million for 47 vulnerabilities at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026, with successful exploits spanning Windows, Linux, VMware, Nvidia, and several AI products. The results highlight ongoing security risks across widely used platforms and enterprise software.

items.title: Meta to bring third-party apps and games to its display glasses
items.summary: Meta is opening up its display glasses to third-party apps and games, which could make the $800 smart glasses substantially more useful. The change appears aimed at making the hardware more compelling as a consumer platform.

items.title: Apple's new Siri app may add auto-deleting chat options
items.summary: Apple is reportedly preparing a revamped Siri app with privacy controls that let users auto-delete chats after 30 days, one year, or never. The update suggests Apple is positioning Siri with stronger privacy features as it refreshes its assistant strategy.

items.title: SpaceX reportedly preparing for a possible IPO as early as June
items.summary: Reuters, via Engadget, reports that SpaceX could make an initial public offering announcement as soon as next week. If confirmed, it would be one of the biggest public-market tech events of the year.

items.title: Tesla robotaxi safety concerns continue after new crash data
items.summary: New crash data indicates Tesla's robotaxi program has had two incidents since July 2025 involving remote operators. The update raises fresh questions about the pace of autonomy deployment and operational safety.
